2604.23970.title: LLM-Guided Agentic Floor Plan Parsing for Accessible Indoor Navigation of Blind and Low-Vision People
2604.23970.paper_summary: Indoor navigation remains a critical accessibility challenge for the blind and low-vision (BLV) individuals, as existing solutions rely on costly per-building infrastructure. We present an agentic framework that converts a single floor plan image into a structured, retrievable knowledge base to generate safe, accessible navigation instructions with lightweight infrastructure. The system has two phases: a multi-agent module that parses the floor plan into a spatial knowledge graph through a self-correcting pipeline with iterative retry loops and corrective feedback; and a Path Planner that generates accessible navigation instructions, with a Safety Evaluator agent assessing potential hazards along each route. We evaluate the system on the real-world UMBC Math and Psychology building (floors MP-1 and MP-3) and on the CVC-FP benchmark. On MP-1, we achieve success rates of 92.31%, 76.92%, and 61.54% for short, medium, and long routes, outperforming the strongest single-call baseline (Claude 3.7 Sonnet) at 84.62%, 69.23%, and 53.85%. On MP-3, we reach 76.92%, 61.54%, and 38.46%, compared to the best baseline at 61.54%, 46.15%, and 23.08%. These results show consistent gains over single-call LLM baselines and demonstrate that our workflow is a scalable solution for accessible indoor navigation for BLV individuals.
2604.23970.paper_summary_zh: 室內導航對盲人和低視力（BLV）個體來說仍然是一個關鍵的可及性挑戰，因為現有的解決方案依賴於昂貴的每棟建築基礎設施。<lb/>我們提出了一個代理框架，將單一樓層平面圖像轉換為結構化的可檢索知識庫，以生成安全、可及的導航指示，並使用輕量級基礎設施。<lb/>該系統有兩個階段：一個多代理模塊，通過自我校正的管道和迭代重試循環及校正反饋，將樓層平面圖解析為空間知識圖；以及一個路徑規劃器，生成可及的導航指示，並由安全評估代理評估每條路徑上的潛在危險。<lb/>我們在現實世界的UMBC數學與心理學大樓（MP-1和MP-3樓層）以及CVC-FP基準上評估該系統。<lb/>在MP-1上，我們的成功率分別為92.31%、76.92%和61.54%，對於短、中和長路徑，超越了最強的單次調用基線（Claude 3.7 Sonnet）的84.62%、69.23%和53.85%。<lb/>在MP-3上，我們達到76.92%、61.54%和38.46%，相比之下最佳基線為61.54%、46.15%和23.08%。<lb/>這些結果顯示出相對於單次調用LLM基線的一致增長，並證明我們的工作流程是為BLV個體提供可及室內導航的可擴展解決方案。
2604.23970.author: Aydin Ayanzadeh et.al.
